mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding real economic activity, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the near term. This effect is always present, as production growth consistently supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 101.5055 is recovering from pandemic trough
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO 1-month change vs 20-day forward return is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in months with rising INDPRO are not significantly above average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits. When the decline exceeds a threshold, the effect is especially pronounced as it confirms the end of the recessionary phase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 6.9 in latest month, elevated from pre-pandemic lows
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE drops >0.5pp MoM, the median 5-day forward return is not positive (sign test p > 0.10), or the proportion of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based consumer and housing demand strength, which leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns. The joint signal is more reliable than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS post positive month-over-month changes
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST 1415 and RSAFS 549256 are both well above pandemic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint positive change dummy vs 20-day forward return is below 0.04 over 2-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive forward returns following joint increases is not above 55% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery regime, rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) from elevated unemployment levels confirm the expansion is sustainable, lifting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 142.373 million is recovering but still below pre-pandemic peak
failure_condition: Within the post-recession recovery regime, the Rank IC of PAYEMS 3-month change vs 60-day forward return is below 0.03, or the mean forward return in periods of rising PAYEMS is not significantly above the mean during declines (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above a threshold after a period of near-zero rates, it signals the start of a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In windows where the trigger fires, the median 20-day forward return is not negative (sign test p > 0.10), or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When both real GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) are rising sequentially, it confirms synchronized expansion in output across the economy. This interaction suggests broad-based earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both post positive sequential (QoQ or MoM) changes
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18583.984 is recovering from Q2 2020 trough
failure_condition: The joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean 60-day forward return after joint increases is not greater than after joint decreases (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of future cash flows and forces the central bank to taper asset purchases, which negatively impacts equity valuations. The effect is most pronounced when the Fed funds rate is below 1%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.325 suggests moderate inflation acceleration
failure_condition: After the trigger fires, the median 60-day forward return is not negative (sign test p > 0.10), or the rank IC of CPI YoY growth vs forward return is below 0.03 in the low-rate regime over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong week in retail sales (RSAFS) signals immediate consumer spending momentum, which directly boosts near-term corporate cash flows and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 549256 is above pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: After the trigger fires, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns is less than 60% (binomial test p > 0.05), or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year backtest.
